components.name: AlertDemoApiSpecComponent
components.templateData:
Properties

Property Type Default Value Description
type AlertType 'info' The type of the alert. Possible values are success, info, warning, and error.
message string '' The message to be displayed in the alert.
size AlertSize 'medium' The size of the alert. Possible values are small, medium, and large.
actionText string '' (Optional) The text for the action button. If provided, an action button will be displayed.
closeable boolean true (Optional) If set to false, the alert will not be closeable. Default is true.

Events

Event Description
close Emits when the alert is closed.
action Emits when the action button is clicked.

CSS Custom Properties

Css Custom Property Description
--alert-success-background-color Success alert background color
--alert-success-border-color Success alert border color
--alert-success-text-color Success alert font color
--alert-info-background-color Info alert background color
--alert-info-border-color Info alert border color
--alert-info-text-color Info alert font color
--alert-warning-background-color Warning alert background color
--alert-warning-border-color Warning alert border color
--alert-warning-text-color Warning alert font color
--alert-error-background-color Error alert background color
--alert-error-border-color Error alert border color
--alert-error-text-color Error alert font color

components.name: AlertDemoComponent
components.templateData:
Use to display alert messages with different types (success, info, warning, error), sizes (small, medium, large), and optional actions.

Use When

  • Displaying important messages to users
  • Providing feedback after actions
  • Highlighting important information

Behavior

  • Supports multiple alert types: success, info, warning, error
  • Supports multiple sizes: small, medium, large (default is medium)
  • Allows custom actions with an optional action button
  • The alert can be closed using the close icon (default is closeable)

Import the AlertComponent in your app.module.ts and use it via <mad-alert> .

Basic usage with optional values

API Specification

components.name: BasePageLayoutComponent
components.templateData:
Following example shows a base page layout without multiple pages. If you have different pages (different header, ...) with routing see next example.

Design Guidelines

All pages in an application should follow a common layout structure to ensure a consistent user experience across applications.

The Base Pagelayout includes the following components:

  • Main Navigation
  • Pagetitle
  • Content Area

Further components may complete the pagelayout as needed:

  • Back Button
  • Command bar
  • Content-Panel

Command Bar

  • The Command Bar comprises the most common users tasks.
  • The most important ones are shown, additional ones are accessible in an overflow-menu.
  • Other commands, either less common ones or where the proximity to an ui element is crucial, are provided within the content area.

Demo

Open Base Pagelayout Demo

Import the CardModule in your app.module.ts and use it via <mad-card>

components.templateData:
(The DataTableComponent will replace the TableComponent in future versions)

Tables are for organizing large volumes of data that users can scan, compare and perform actions on.

Please find the main documentation here: Material Design table
In the following only additional features are documented

Import the DataTableModule in your app.module.ts and use it via: <mad-data-table>

Examples:

  • Basic Table: Basic table with pagination and filter
  • Data transformation: Custom transformation of column data
  • Single Mode: Table with single mode actions
  • Batch Mode: Table with batch mode actions
  • Async Loading: Table with asynchronus loading (sorting, filtering and paging handled on the server side)
  • Row ID: Return IDs instead of full rows on events
  • Child rows: Use child rows (limited support)
  • Column configuration: Allow the user to view and edit different column configurations
  • Table height: Stretch table to parent height

At the end of the page you will find a summary table of all possible inputs and outputs to use as a cheat sheet.

Basic table

The datatable expects the following input:

  • displayedColumns: definitions of columns of type DataTableColumnHeader with the following properties
    • label (required): label displayed in the table (will be translated if possible)
    • dataPropertyName (required): property name of the column in the list of data
    • isSortable (optional): if true, column will be sortable if false (or null), column will not be sortable
    • isRightAligned (optional): if true, column will have its text aligned to the right, if false (or null) to the left
    • transformer (optional): if a function is provided, data will be transformed before being displayed (see Data Transformation Example below). If null, data will be displayed as is
    • transformerParams (optional): params for transformer function
Back to overview

Data transformation

To transform the displayed data you can set the optional properties transformer and transformerParams in the column definition. You can supply any function as transformer.

  • Example: Custom transformations for the columns Title (translation), Gender (symbol) and Email (uppercase)
  • notice that the internal sort and filter will always use the displayed data, not the actual data. (E.g. you will no longer find matches for 'Mr' or 'Mrs' but instead for 'Herr' or 'Frau'.)
  • events (like row actions) will still always return the actual data
Back to overview

Single mode

If at least one rowAction exists with type SINGLE (but no batch action) the table will be displayed in single mode by default.

  • All row actions will be available in the action menu of the leftmost column
  • Clicking on any row trigger the default action (=the first action)
  • Actions with type NONE are actions that are independent of rows and will be displayed above the table (example: "CREATE" action)
Back to overview

Batch mode (multiselect)

If at least one rowAction exists with type BATCH the table will be displayed in batch mode by default. You can forcefully override the display mode of the table (see example below with button 'BATCH SELECTION' to force switching between BATCH and SINGLE).

  • Clicking on any row will select or unselected the row.
  • All rowActions will be displayed above the table (SINGLE, BATCH and NONE).
  • The buttons will be disabled if the amount of selected rows does not match the rowAction type of SINGLE (1), BATCH (>0) or NONE (any amount allowed, selection will be ignored)
  • Batch actions will have an indicator showing the number of selected rows. SINGLE and NONE have no indicators, as SINGLE actions always affect one row and NONE actions are always independent of selections.
  • Clicking the checkbox in the header will select all rows of the current page.
Back to overview

Asynchronous loading

Use asynchronous loading if the dataset is very large (so you only load a single page of the data).

  • As the table no longer has all data (only one page of it), it cannot perform any of the following tasks in a meaningful way: filtering, sorting, paging
  • filtering: the internal filter is always deactivated; [filterEnabled] will be ignored and is always false; you can create your own filter and load the data accordingly, then update [page] and [tableData]
  • sorting: subscribe to sorting changes with (sortEvent), load the new page of data from the server, then update [page] and [tableData]
  • paging: set the current page with [page] and subscribe to paging changes with (pageEvent), load the new page of data from the server, then update [page] and [tableData]
  • While performing tasks you can display a spinner animation by setting [loading] to true
Back to overview

IDs instead of rows

By default, row actions will return the complete selected rows (actual data, not displayed data) in the event.
If you want events to return IDs instead of full row objects, provide an ID generator function to [idGenerator] as in the example below.

Back to overview

Child rows (limited support)

It is now possible to mark rows as child rows. For now, child rows only have limited support:

  • no table actions for child rows (ignored in batch and single actions)
  • automatic sorting will not work properly (implement own sorting logic or use asnyc datatable with correct sorting in the backend)
  • provide [idGenerator] and [parentIdGenerator]. All rows having a generated parentId will be treated as child rows
  • if more features for child rows are required, feel free to add an issue... or even better: help and create a pull request!
Back to overview

Custom column configuration

If you provide at least one template for [columnDefinitionTemplates] (default: []), the table will display a button for the user to configure the columns. The template may have a name (if not the name will be USER) and must have a list of all available columns, and all selected columns. On clicking the button a modal will open where the user can chose their selected columns and reorder them with three available actions: SAVE, RESET, and CANCEL.

The dataTable will return a (columnConfigurationChanged) event with the name of the action, the name of the edited columnDefinitionTemplate (default: USER) and the selected configuration. Subscribe to this event if you want to persist the user's configuration to your storage or database.

The dataTable will return a (columnConfigurationChanged) event with the name of the action and the selected configuration. Subscribe to this event if you want to persist the user's configuration to your storage or database.

If you provide more than one ColumnDefinitionTemplate, the user can choose which template they want to edit.

Back to overview

Stretch table to parent height

Set [tableClass] to datatable-vertical-stretch-to-parent to have the table stretch to the height of its parent (to fill a card or the whole content area. Note that the table has a minimum height and will get a scollbar if it cannot display all of its rows so that the pagination will always be visible.

Back to overview

Filter table based on columns

Use [filterMode] to COLUMN_BASED to filter a table based on its columns. Furthermore, add the isFilterable property to each DataTableColumn, which is filterable. The filterParams property filterOptions can be used to pass an array of key-value pairs to filter on a predefined set (e.g. enum values).

Back to overview

Stateful datatable

Set [persistenceConfig] to persistSort: true, persistFilter: true, persistePageSize: true to persist the datatables sort, filter and pageSize state. Override the DataTablePersistenceService class and inject it via DATA_TABLE_PERSISTENCE_SERVICE_PROVIDER to provide a custom implementation.

Back to overview

Cell templates for custom cell definitions

Use madColumnDef to define a new columnDefinition using the id of the DataTableColumn. Use madCellDef to define a new cellDefinition for the corresponding madColumnDef. Use the let element syntax inside the madCellDef directive to retrieve the element of the current row, to access its data.

Back to overview

Template to make a row expandable

Use madExpandableColumnDef to define a new expandableDefinition. Use madExpandableCellDef to define a new cellDefinition for the corresponding madExpandableColumnDef. Use the let element syntax inside the madExpandableCellDef directive to retrieve the element of the current row, to access its data. Use [rowExpandable] to define a function that marks rows as expandable, true by default.

Back to overview

Summary of all possible inputs and outputs

The following table displays an overview of all possible input variables (to pass data to the table) and output events (to subscribe to).

  • Name: name of the variable
  • Input / output: input variable or output event
  • Type: expected type of input or output (all classes are either provided with typescript, angular material or with the table)
  • Sync / async: marks variables or events that are only useful for either synchronous or asynchronous loading
  • Default value: value of the input variable (if no other value is provided)
  • Description: Short description. For more information see examples above
Back to overview

components.name: FlowbarPageLayoutComponent
components.templateData:
The Flow Bar Layout extends the Base Page Layout by adding a wizard-like navigation control.

This layout that can be used to highlight the current progress a user is making throughout multiple steps. The Flow Bar allows the user to navigate between steps, by either clicking on a specific step or using the previous or next button.

Usage

Use this layout if you want to guide the user through a sequence of multiple steps. Each step requires the user to perform a complex task like filling out a form. Don't use this layout if the user's steps are mutually exclusive; in this case use Tabs instead.

When implementing a flow bar on your page, you will need to insert a mad-flowbar right below your content-header but above your content-container.

Following inputs & outputs are available:

  • [steps] - an array of FlowBarStep. You can extend/implement this interface to save even more information in your steps.
  • [(activeStep)] two way binding of the active step.

You can use the @ViewChild() annotation to get a reference of your FlowBarComponent, which will allow you to call its methods:

  • previous() - open the previous step.
  • next() - opens the next step.
  • isPreviousAvailable() - returns true if the previous step can be opened.
  • isNextAvailable() - returns true if the next step can be opened.
  • isLastStep() - returns true if the current step is the last.

Demo

Open Base Pagelayout Demo with a Flow Bar

components.templateData:
Project Material-Addons is an addon to Angular Material which is based on the Material Design system.

It contains additional components and patterns which are not in scope of Angular Material and also provides customized themes.

🎉 Material Design 3 (M3) Support

Version 22.0.0+ fully supports Material Design 3:

  • Modern M3 color system and design tokens
  • Improved accessibility and contrast
  • All existing CSS variables preserved
  • No breaking API changes

View Migration Guide →

How to Use Material-Addons

Step 0: Install Material

Material-Addons requires an already set-up Angular Material project. To do a fresh start please follow the official Angular Material guide before you continue, but exclude step 4 ("include a theme").

Requirements: Angular 21+ and Angular Material 21+ for Material-Addons v22.x

Step 1: Install Material-Addons package

npm install @porscheinformatik/material-addons@22.0.0 --save

Step 2: Add a theme stylesheet at the top of your project's styles.scss

@use 'node_modules/@porscheinformatik/material-addons/themes/poa.scss';

Available themes: poa.scss (cyan), pbv.scss (dark blue), carcat.scss (light blue)
All themes support Material Design 3 from v22.0.0+

Step 3: Add Material-Addons component to Angular application

To use a component, you need to import the Module of the component in your app.module.ts or in any other module, which needs the component.



Contributing to Material-Addons

The Material-Addons team welcomes contributions from the community

Internal Porsche Informatik Guidelines can be found here: Porsche Informatik Guidelines

© {{ currentYear() }} by Porsche Informatik GmbH

Following example shows a sidebar page layout with a collapsable sidebar container that can hold different navigation entries.

Design Guidelines

All pages in an application should follow a common layout structure to ensure a consistent user experience across applications.

The Base SidebarLayout includes the following components:

  • Same components as the base page layout
  • plus a collapsable navigation panel on the right hand side
  • the default text color used for the links is #900 from the primary-palette of the theme. As an alternative you can define your own '--palette-color' variable

Demo

Open Sidebar Pagelayout Demo

The Advanced Sidebar Layout With Custom Header (mad-sidebar-layout) integrates a flexible integration system with customizable header, sidebar, content and footer sections.

Design Guidelines

The Advanced Sidebar Layout With Custom Header works with Angular Portals and includes the following components:

  • headerContent - Customizable header content
  • sideBarItems - Navigation items for the sidebar
  • content - Main content area where router components are displayed
  • footer - Footer area

Demo

The Advanced Sidebar Layout With Default Header (mad-sidebar-layout) offers a variation of the sidebar layout that includes a default header with an additional action group.

Design Guidelines

The Advanced Sidebar Layout With Default Header works with Angular Portals and includes the following components:

  • actionGroup - Default header action buttons content
  • title - Default header title
  • hasBackButton - Default header back button appearance
  • content - Main content area where router components are displayed

components.name: StepperDemoComponent
components.templateData:
A stepper allows to create workflows that divides content into logical steps

This stepper component is based on the CdkStepper implementation.
Please find the documentation here: CdkStepper


Use When

  • work processes may not fit in one viewport
  • creation of workflows and separation of content into logical steps is needed
  • there is a linear workflow

Behavior

  • initially the first step is open
  • steps can be accessed either through the navigation button inside the step or by clicking on the step header
  • moving to the next step is only possible when the current step is completed/valid
  • steps are highlighted if they are valid/completed or invalid

Linear stepper

The stepper only allows a linear processing of the steps. The user has to complete the previous steps before proceeding. For each step, the stepControl attribute has to be set which is used to check the validity of the step.


Navigation

The stepper implementation already takes care of the navigation of the stepper. A step can either be accessed by the button inside the step or by clicking on the header. Alternatively, there is the possibility to define the navigation by using onNext/onDone/onHeaderClick. Then the navigation has to be triggered manually. You can use the @ViewChild() annotation to get a reference of your StepperComponent, which allows you to call the following method:

  • next() - navigate to the next step

Import the StepperModule in your app.module.ts and use it via <mad-stepper> .

components.templateData:
The toolbar component's data is set via a service. Therefore, each toolbar on this site has the same data.

Title handling

You are able to set the title of the toolbar via: this.toolbarService.toolbarTitle = "Toolbar Demo Title"; If no title is specified, the website title (e.g. on this site it's Toolbar Component) will be set.

Additionally, you can set a sub title via: this.toolbarService.setDataTitle("Sub title");

Toolbar actions

Three actions are available in the toolbar:

  • Main Action
  • Toolbar Action
  • Back Action

Main Action

The main action renders an icon and a text in the middle of the toolbar. We recommend to use not more than two main actions. The following properties are available:

Toolbar Action

Every toolbar action renders an icon button with an hint on hover. It appears on the far right site of the toolbar. The following properties are available:

Back Action

You can specify a back action via: this.toolbarService.addBackAction('/home'); This will render a back arrow in front of the toolbar title, which navigates to the specified route.

It's also possible to define an absolute route to a different website by setting the 'isAbsoluteUrl' flag true: this.toolbarService.addBackAction('https://www.example.com', true); The same back arrow as above will appear, this time using a [href] instead of a [routerLink].

components.name: UploadDemoApiSpecComponent
components.templateData:
Properties

Property Type Default Value Description
id string '' HTML id of the element.
multiple boolean if false only 1 document can be added, otherwise you can add as much documents as you want.
accept string[] '[]' is a string array which contains all valid document types.
text string 'Upload' Text in the Upload Field.
showFileList boolean false Shows the list of added files.
removable boolean true Uploaded file can be deleted using icon in chip.

Events

Event Description
fileEmitter Emits when file is uploaded/removed. Contains file list
errorEmitter (ErrorType) ONLY_SINGLE_FILE: if only 1 File is allowed and user tries to add more than 1 File in 1 dialog.
errorEmitter (ErrorType) FILETYPE_NOT_SUPPORTED: if user tries to add unsupported file type

components.name: UploadDemoComponent
components.templateData:
The Upload Component allows you to upload 1 to n documents


Behavior

  • Clickable with dialog to select files
  • Drag And Drop Functionality
  • Replace file with a new one in single upload mode

Output

  • fileEmitter: FileList with all files in it
  • errorEmitter: ErrorType
    • ONLY_SINGLE_FILE: if only 1 File is allowed and user tries to add more than 1 File
    • FILETYPE_NOT_SUPPORTED: if user tries to add unsupported file type

FileUploadComponent in your app.module.ts and use it via <mad-file-upload>

API Specification

components.name: UploadFileComponent
components.templateData:
<mat-form-field>
  <mat-label>Supported File Types</mat-label>
  <mat-select [(ngModel)]="accept" multiple>
    @for (possibleFileEnding of possibleFileEndings; track possibleFileEnding) {
      <mat-option [value]="possibleFileEnding">{{ possibleFileEnding }}</mat-option>
    }
  </mat-select>
</mat-form-field>

<mat-checkbox [(ngModel)]="multiple">{{ 'Allow Multiple Uploads' }}</mat-checkbox>
<mat-checkbox [(ngModel)]="showFileList">{{ 'Show File List' }}</mat-checkbox>
<mat-checkbox [(ngModel)]="removable">{{ 'Removable File' }}</mat-checkbox>

<mad-file-upload
  [accept]="accept"
  [multiple]="multiple"
  [text]="'Upload File'"
  [showFileList]="showFileList"
  [removable]="removable"
  [id]="'upload'"
  (fileEmitter)="filesEmitted($event)"
  (errorEmitter)="errorReceived($event)"
/>
